name: Bialys
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Secrets of a Jewish Baker, George Greenstein
1. Combine topping ingredients and set aside.
2. In a large bowl sprinkle yeast over the warm water to soften; stir to dissolve. Add sugar, 6 cups flour, and salt. Mix thoroughly until dough forms up and comes away from the sides of the bowl.
3. Turn out the dough onto a floured work surface and knead, adding small amounts of flour as needed, for 10 to 12 minutes.
4. Shape the dough into a ball; place in a large oiled bowl and turn to coat. Cover and allow to rise 30 minutes. Press out all of the air with your fingers and allow to rise until doubled in size (20-30 minutes).
5. Punch down the dough, divide into thirds, roll out under palms into ropes, and cut each rope into 6 equal pieces. Roll into balls. Cover and allow the dough to rest for 10 minutes.
6. Rollout each ball into a 3 1/2 inch circle. If the dough becomes too stiff or shrinks back, allow it to rest and go on to the next piece.
7. Evenly space the circles on 2 floured or cornmeal-dusted baking pans. Cover with flour-rubbed cloths and allow to rise until puffy.
8. Make an indentation from the center outward, leaving a 1-inch rim. A shot glass with a 1-inch bottom works well. Press with a circular motion.
9. Dribble bit of reserved topping into the hole. Dust lightly with reserved flour. Cover with cloths and allow to proof until puffed up.
10. Bake without steam in a preheated 450°F oven for 15-20 minutes.
Make 18 bialys.
